Wire routes for sign-in redirect, user display, and sign-out

dotnet.callout: Server-side flow — the client_secret never reaches the browser. PKCE is still enabled to protect against authorization-code interception during the redirect back to /callback.

steps.description: Declarative OIDC client registration. spring-dotenv resolves ${CLIENT_ID}, ${CLIENT_SECRET}, ${ISSUER_URL}, ${SCOPES} from the .env file at the sample root.
steps.code:
spring:
  security:
    oauth2:
      client:
        registration:
          secureauth:
            client-id: "{{CLIENT_ID}}"
            client-secret: "{{CLIENT_SECRET}}"
            client-authentication-method: client_secret_post
            scope: "{{SCOPES}}"
            authorization-grant-type: authorization_code
            redirect-uri: "{baseUrl}/login/oauth2/code/{registrationId}"
        provider:
          secureauth:
            issuer-uri: "{{ISSUER_URL}}"
            user-name-attribute: sub

steps.description: Spring Boot entry point — auto-configures the OAuth2 client, web server, and security filter chain.
SecurityFilterChain enables oauth2Login() for Authorization Code + PKCE and wires RP-initiated logout via OidcClientInitiatedLogoutSuccessHandler.
Home renders a signed-out page with a Sign in link when no OidcUser is present, and renders the signed-in page using claims from OidcUser otherwise.

java.callout: Server-side flow — the client_secret never reaches the browser. PKCE is still enabled to protect against authorization-code interception during the redirect back to /login/oauth2/code/secureauth.

steps.description: Import the ASP.NET Core OIDC/cookie middleware plus Duende.IdentityModel (used later for the refresh-token grant) and DotNetEnv for `.env` loading
Configure cookie auth + OpenID Connect; wire OnValidatePrincipal so every authenticated request can auto-refresh the access token
Swap a refresh token for a fresh access token via the OIDC token endpoint
Routes: user display, sign-in, manual refresh (POST /refresh), sign-out
Auto-refresh via the cookie auth OnValidatePrincipal event — runs on every authenticated request

dotnet.callout: Requires refresh_token grant type enabled in workspace [OAuth settings](workspace-oauth-general) and in the application's [Grant Types](workspace-applications-oauth). Also requires the offline_access scope.

steps.description: Declarative OIDC client registration with offline_access scope so SecureAuth issues refresh tokens. spring-dotenv resolves ${CLIENT_ID}, ${CLIENT_SECRET}, ${ISSUER_URL}, ${SCOPES} from the .env file at the sample root.
steps.code:
spring:
  security:
    oauth2:
      client:
        registration:
          secureauth:
            client-id: "{{CLIENT_ID}}"
            client-secret: "{{CLIENT_SECRET}}"
            client-authentication-method: client_secret_post
            scope: "{{SCOPES}}"
            authorization-grant-type: authorization_code
            redirect-uri: "{baseUrl}/login/oauth2/code/{registrationId}"
        provider:
          secureauth:
            issuer-uri: "{{ISSUER_URL}}"
            user-name-attribute: sub

steps.description: Spring Boot entry point — same shape as the login-auth-code sample; imports Spring Security's OAuth2 client + authorized-client APIs used below for refresh.
SecurityFilterChain enables oauth2Login() for Authorization Code + PKCE and wires RP-initiated logout via OidcClientInitiatedLogoutSuccessHandler.

OAuth2AuthorizedClientManager enables Spring's background auto-refresh on any authorized-client use. A separate RefreshTokenOAuth2AuthorizedClientProvider bean is injected into RefreshController for on-demand refresh from POST /refresh.
Home renders the signed-in page, reading the access-token expiry from the stored OAuth2AuthorizedClient and surfacing a POST /refresh button.

POST /refresh loads the current OAuth2AuthorizedClient, builds an OAuth2AuthorizationContext, and invokes RefreshTokenOAuth2AuthorizedClientProvider.authorize(...) directly — forcing the refresh grant regardless of token expiry. On failure, renders an inline error page with the IdP's error code.

java.callout: Requires refresh_token grant type enabled in workspace [OAuth settings](workspace-oauth-general) and in the application's [Grant Types](workspace-applications-oauth). Also requires the offline_access scope.

steps.description: Discover the OIDC provider and configure the client
Build the authorization URL with PKCE and a CSRF state
Exchange the authorization code for tokens (access + refresh + id token + expiry)
Swap a refresh token for a fresh access token (and possibly a rotated refresh token)
Wire the OIDC helpers into Express routes with a server-managed session and POST /refresh

steps.description: Configure cookie auth + SAML2 SP against your SecureAuth workspace.
Wire routes for sign-in challenge, signed-in display, and local sign-out.

dotnet.callout: AuthnRequests are unsigned for simplicity — CIAM accepts unsigned requests by default. For production, configure SP request signing per your library's docs.

steps.description: Configure the relying-party registration with CIAM as the SAML IdP
Enable saml2Login and accept unsolicited (IdP-initiated) responses
Render the home page — signed-in shows the SAML NameID, otherwise a sign-in link

java.callout: Spring Security 6.4 always signs AuthnRequests; this sample reuses the dev TLS keystore as the SP signing credential. CIAM doesn't validate the signature (no SP cert registered), so the request is accepted as if unsigned. For production, generate a dedicated SP signing key and register its public cert in CIAM's SAML SP form.

steps.description: Configure passport-saml with SecureAuth as the SAML IdP
Initiate SP-initiated SSO — redirect the user to SecureAuth's SAML SSO endpoint
Validate the SAML response at the ACS endpoint and establish the session — the same handler accepts both SP-initiated and IdP-initiated responses (validateInResponseTo: "ifPresent")

node.callout: AuthnRequests are unsigned for simplicity — CIAM accepts unsigned requests by default. For production, configure SP request signing per your library's docs.

Open the system browser, run Auth Code + PKCE, and receive the tokens

react-native.callout: Uses the system browser (ASWebAuthenticationSession on iOS / Custom Tabs on Android) — never an embedded WebView. AppAuth enforces this for you.

steps.description: Import AppAuth, env loader, and the secure-storage backend
Configure the OIDC client — the offline_access scope (set in .env) enables refresh tokens
Trigger login with the offline_access scope and capture the refresh token
Persist the refresh token in platform secure storage (Keychain on iOS, EncryptedSharedPreferences on Android)
Exchange the refresh token for a new access token; on failure clear local state and require re-login
